content: it skips trailers after 3 seconds, really annoying any time you're trying to watch a game's trailer. definitely prevented me from buying some games recently. also on the pc version, i've been begging them for years to make it easier to browse our own library. for over 10 years i've been asking and waiting. i get that all the development went towards store pages, but it's like 4 clicks to see what genre a game even is from my library. that's super tedious and feel horrible when you're in big pic

first, the fact the chat is split into a second application is daft. absolutely ridiculous. second, it logged me out, seemingly spontaneously, and asked for the code to log me back in, the code from the app that logged me out, that code, which was clearly inaccessible. if the app isn't going to stay consistently active on a device, it shouldn't be requiring 2fa from itself. using my phone number to reset was smooth enough admittedly, but the whole thing feels absurd.

it's pretty damn hard to use this...website on mobile is easier and more consistent. wishlist is a great example - vertically you can only view and not rearrange or change the order -- horizontally you can tap in the number field as if to type a new wishlist number order but no keys ever appear, and you can tap and drag technically (like on desktop) but it is so difficult to do correctly because the first time you drag it, the whole page moves oddly, so you have to re-grab and get lucky.

while i love steam this on one of the **worst** official mobile ports of anything i've ever seen. it's like everything is hidden. once you pull up the keyboard by searching for a game. there is no way to get rid of it. i was trying to add a game to my cart. i figured it out but while trying i tried to add it through the wishlist. in the wishlist there's a cart button next to the game. it does nothing.

if they could figure out the bugs this app would be alright. at least 50% of the time i get an error rather than the link i clicked on. i just had a notification for something on my wish list on sale. i clicked on it and got "there was a problem loading this web content." after that nothing worked. other times i get, "there was a problem signing you in."

works well in securing your account (provided you are only attempting to do one account per smart phone, i ran into issues trying to have 2 accounts on the same cell), as well as buying games on the fly & receiving chat messages when not at your pc (reading is another matter), but there are a number of issues with the app that makes it better to stick to doing those tasks on your pc. trailers (on store pages) do not correctly work in full screen on this app, nor do listings of discussions on their boards (the individual discussions load fine, but good luck getting to them if you don't have a direct link in chat or a notification of a reply to one of those threads). chat sometimes also has an issue where it will glitch violently while the other person is typing, making it pretty much impossible to read what has been said while they are still typing; at the very least, you know they sent you a message, but you might have to go to your pc to read/reply to it...

mostly unusable for anything but browsing new games from the home screen. search is missing for anything but games (can't search workshop etc), back button is broken and throws you to the home page no matter where you are on the site, several features like friends are gone... honestly it's more hassle than it's worth most of the time. it resembles the website, but doesn't come even remotely close to what you can do on the web.

app only sorta works. you can mostly navigate it, its decent if you want notifications for your wishlisted games going on sale, but god forbid you try buying them on the app. it just will not let you, it starts an endless cycle of making you reenter your login credentials, verify the code from your email, rinse and repeat with no end. its like they dont want me to give them money. and good luck navigating this godforsaken app, the cart is nowhere to be found, and every function is hidden.

when i tried creating an account, i couldnt create a password. every time i tried, it would take me to the sign in page although i didnt have an account. none of the help options would make things any easier, if not make things worse. the account recovery is just as difficult. after thirty minutes, it wouldnt send the recovery email, and after i tried again, i had used all the recovery attempts and my account name was apparently invalid.

found a fix: had to navigate to steam guard and sign in again (even though i was already signed in and shouldn't have had to). then, i suddenly got all new pages and ui fetched down to my latest edition of the app. before it was using the old ui even though it was the latest app edition. updated rating to ** until an update fixes things can't do anything that requires authentication despite account being signed in. i can't add anything to my wishlist, for instance, which makes the app unusable.

was perfectly fine before the update... the app was a great way to have mobile access to the store and a glance at current games in-library. the app just went through a large update that changed most items and cosmetics. the library view of games is now way worse - before, there was a nice list of games with information. now, there is an array of game images. this means that 1) the order of games is harder to follow because it's a grid and 2) that you have to click on them to see stats. :(

i've been using steam for along time and it is good on alot of stuff. except when i am downloading a game and it get so close to being finished with a download then i have to restart my computer for some other reason, my download starts over and i have start the update all the way from the beginning. this has been a problem for years and still has not been fixed. it's something that needs to be fixed.

frustrated — i very often cannot purchase or add to my wallet from my steam app - i normally use paypal and every time i'm trying to check out, the paypal option just glitches and has me re-enter my email over and over again. it's a big loss when i'm away from home and want to make a purchase bc i see a sale notification but can't, then i put the phone down and forget and then i just never buy the game (also the sale will end by the time i remember). please get your checkout together.

update: i keep getting "webpage not available" and "there is a problem loading this web content" errors despite a stable connection. update: getting better, but still takes a while to load webpages, or i sometimes get errors on certain pages and have to reload. buggy, but my previous issue is not an issue any more. will update once i use it more.

it's useful for chatting with steam friends or looking/buying from the catalog while away from the pc. one thing i would like to see is an feature to redeem keys via the app so i don't have to get to a computer with the steam client already installed. so far i haven't found a way to do that. update: it seems they don't maintain the app to work on newer phones. at this point it's probably better to just use steam on your phone's browser. you really only need the app for authentication purposes.

i can't even get into steam, as the email doesn't even send to me for authentication, even after getting emails from steam support after spending money. my other complaint is that i have to wait a whole week to spend my $100 on the community market, and there doesn't seem to be a way to remove this restriction, and i can't seem to find any real reason this restriction exists.

i guess i was too quick to say that the chat bug has been fixed. the app still frequently does weird things where chat messages don't display until you close your conversation and reopen it. this seems to happen whenever i have steam running on my pc at home. it is better than it used to be, but it's still not working as well as i'd like it. this paired with the fact that i don't use the majority of the features that are present on this app have made me uninstall it. my friends and myself have decided to switch to discord for chatting when we're not using steam at home. it's a shame because i liked having steam be a one stop shop for everything. hopefully you will get this fixed and add some other compelling reasons to use steams mobile app in the future valve. :(

much like the traditional steam experience, this app is more than a little clunky to use. there are a lot of ui features that are frustrating to navigate on a phone. at the very least, i would like i didn't get a "download now"/"install steam to access your purchase" when i buy new games. one redeeming quality is that you can get notifications when games on your wishlist are on sale, in case you've ever wanted to buy games from the office.

overall design is good, but backspace closes the app, and clicking on the icon doesnt refresh your options. meaning you can get locked out of your inventory while doing trades, only solution is to go off wifi as its ip locked. sell all items is clickable but does not interact. chat being seperate is a chore. the bugs are atrocious but the design is better.

install to pc is an awesome feature! loading links from firefox sometimes fails. click link for a game (or community page?) and steam app opens but shows the last game i looked at. unfortunately, the back button is completely broken. look at a game, read a review, and use the android back button sends me back to my library instead of to the game's store page. seems like it's going back twice, but i'm using gesture back, so i'm obviously not double tapping.

paypal didn't work anymore from the app. i cleared cache / data and then tried to change my password. it wiped steam guard. tried again, set a new password, connect and saw paypal just vanished. the app also asked me to download this very same app to set steam guard. yeah. i'm going to bed.

i love steam but when it comes to the mobile app it was not something that met my expectation. kind of disappointed how there's so much bug in this app. i've been trying to refresh the app since i wanted to check on some games on sale. i even tried to log in using my tab but it always displayed an error. i just hope the bug would be resolved especially that i would mostly use the app for my steam guard.

app has been beneficial towards maintaining and managing steam account. has also been beneficial towards viewing shop and tracking sales or buying from the app and remotely downloading to desktop. recently aop has been unusable to any regard. unable to manage account, shop or and feature. app is logged but no features are available. need to fix.

this app barely works, 2.5 star rating is generous. if you click a game in a category and you want to go back, too bad, there's no back button. you have to press the home icon and get sent to the front page, so you have to search for the category again. everything is drop down menus, so if you're new navigating is just confusing. adult content is fully hidden, regardless of preference settings. most of the issues have existed for years so i'm not holding my breath for it to be fixed.

despite the app being very useful to check sales, redeem gift cards and just browse steam, it is very dangerous. i had the steam guard authenticator activated, with which ypu can approve sign-ins, and when i opened steam one day, my account wasn't signed in. the problem is that with steam guard, sign-ins can only be approved from the mobile phone. thankfully i had all the backup methods on, but i could have easily been locked-out... please fix this sudden logging out thing

recent changes have made the app far less usable. the app now ignores your settings for any form of "adult" content. now if you want to see everything on your list of games with out bluring, even non sexual in nature, you have to log in through the browser. app is essentially just a authenticator at this point.

this app is for people who love having their time wasted arbitrarily.because valve decides to hide things that used to be in plain sight behind a dozen drop down menus. you can also rest assured that they won't update any resources.so you can actually find the options that you're looking for in the app either.

literally preventing me from being able to use my account. i'm sure it must be with some poorly implemented failed authorization check. now it's just doomed to fail and tell the user to try again indefinitely. all because some billionaires can't be bothered to add a couple extra lines of code to prevent the problem. how are you not doing basic defensive coding?

i usually enjoy using this app for saving games without going to my pc. but i always face the problem where i'm signed in, but it says i'm not, and when i try to sign in, it always loads and says server error. it's a dogshit app and not optimized at all. fix your app.

having bad experience with steam on pc and mobile,are mine account has been hacked or what??these 2 or 3 days i've been facing a lot of problem about login and there is some unknown device even try to login my account and i have add the authorize at my phone but it keep showing there is no for my phone.i even get warning by steam with violated for trading,purchasing,gifting all i just do was buying game skin in other trusted website.can you guys give me an explain??

doesnt let me go to my library it says "oops, something went wrong while loading this page." so i cant actually use the app and play the games its useless also i'm guessing theres a 99.9 percent chance they wont respond to this so theres no point anyways
very limited functionality. i can't see what i have already. it seems to only be good for logging into the account on a computer.

i guess you're good but your account recovery sucks, i've tried resetting my password 3 times and you just keep asking me "select an account to recover" i only have 1 account, i've never made a 2nd, and i probably won't, and your "select account" button doesn't even work anyways. i don't know how much i've spent on you but it all feels like a waste now

when playing a video in the storefront, the slide show of photos interrupts after about 3 seconds. the only way to stop the scroll of pictures from interfering is to play the video in full-screen. annoying and it makes me use the storefront less.

2 stars because i was signed out automatically and no i'm not hacked or anything because everything else is working fine. now i can't sign back in because it says my password is incorrect but i know it's right since i have it written down.

tried to buy some games while at work but couldn't get the payment to verify, i don't know why, checked my account and i had funds. tried different payments types, but to no avail. convenient this app is not.

when i tried to go to library in steam and then it says oops, something went wrong while loading this page and i'm a mobile user and i tried these methods to resolve the problem and it didn't work at all such as reinstalling the steam app, rebooting my phone and removing the steam app and rebooted it again my phone and i install it nothing worked and still pops up even when i turn of vpn it's still here please fix this permanent problem for me i do not want factory reset on my phone
all of the sudden the qr sing in stopped working it's one of the main reasons i use the app on my phone. looks like the update from january made some changes to it and that probably broke it.

lots of games but when it comes to buy them the app is very problematic. it claims i have insufficient funds or that my bank has declined the sale even after my bank has approved the sale. def don't recommend anymore

it works okay but payments are really dodgy, sometimes they fail without saying they failed, sometimes they say they failed, sometimes they work with no confirmation, sometimes they work with a confirmation.

it's fine as an authenticator, but it lacks chat for some reason, and the game store is also extremely glitchy, eg, videos won't play in landscape and it will blackscreen. merge chat into this app and then devote resources to one app instead of two buggy ones, please.
when trying to enable steam guard on my steam account, the app tells me they're sending me a code to my phone number. but there's no code sent. kindly look into this.
i tried creating an account but when i went to the email then back to the app, it just restarted and i did it like 5 times and still nothing.
very slow, can't really browse or do much on it. web browser is faster than the app which is kind of crazy. takes forever if it loads at all.
i'm experiencing issues with my password. i just made a new one and i tried to sign in with it after logging out but it doesn't recognize my new password. i will need to remove 3 stars.
having discovery and other queues be a pop-in rather than cycle through full game pages is mad annoying. also if i swipe back that means go back one page, not take me to the main page.
i know i have an unusual phone, but after a few updates i can no longer use my unihertz jelly star as steam guard, the button to authorize it's below the viewport and i can't scroll below.
it won't let me sign in for an hour straight and it just keeps saying failed to log you in, try again later.
works okay except one major bug that blurs graphic or violent content despite having them enabled. please fix this.
this app does not let you play pc games directly on your phone, it’s mainly for account management, browsing, chatting, and security.
i try to add friend in steam but the app shows site is not reached i wish to add some friends please fix the issue
it's a pretty good app but it's so frustrating that you can't zoom into a photo. like, why?
the app is great, when it works! i can't go into my cart, i can't even advance search.
after my initial tap of the app, i get access denied. only after navigating to another section then selecting the menu tab can i get to the main store page.
it works, but it's sooo slow and clunky. it is a pc-centric platform, but this app could do with some optimisation
i purchased a game a few months ago and it's nowhere to be found. it's kind of hard getting support. $16.99 down the drain smh
not as bad as the old app, but still pretty terrible. videos don't even work 90% of times.

the only reason to have this app is when you're away from your computer and want to see and buy the latest games on sale. the most notable aspect is that they send a direct notification to you when a wishlisted game is on sale. managing the app itself is very janky. pressing the back button on your phone would sometimes not bring you back to the front page, and items are not removed from your cart after purchasing them. if you have access to your pc, it's better to just open steam over there.

awful. wishlist and reviews won't load, tapping on "add to wishlist" does nothing, and with the new update i can no longer see my friends' activity. wishlisting could be the most useful feature of a mobile app like this, for when a friend mentions a new game you might have missed and you want to quickly add it lest you forget, but no. you can't even view the screenshots on a game's store page, and they don't seem to want to fix the issue. the only thing it's good for is steamguard.

after the split with steam chat app it caused a big problem: nothing friends related now works with the steam app. seems obvious. you can't view your friends games, you can't see their wishlist, and yes you can't chat with your friends. it's like keeping your milk and butter in two different fridges in different rooms. there were huge problems with the old app that were acknowledged, but it's just really not there yet in terms of user friendliness.

the only thing i want to use this app for like many others is to be able to access, and receive notifications for, steam chat through my phone. there is a bug, that from what i have read on a few different forums has been around for years, where the chat window will not update with new incoming messages until you exit and re-enter that chat. this includes both when the app is in the background and you get an incoming message notification, and when you are actively using the app to chat with a person. you must backout to your friends list and enter chat again or all you will see is the messages you have sent. ontop of that nicknames that you set to keep track of people that like to change name/pictures alot are not visible in the apps friends list. it is only displayed on each friends profile page.
the app says i need to download steam guard and then redirects me to the page to download the app that just redirected me there... i need to download the app i already have. how....
qr code to verify a device doesnt work and after many attempts to change my password im now locked out of my account

app says it needs access to camera to use steam guard. i already provided access in "ask every time" mode. but app forces me to give 24/7 access to my camera! so stupid devs or company policy compromising user security!???

pretty useless app. the app seems to have many similar features to the desktop version for only 7mb storage place, but most of the features are broken. based on my experience you can't view storepages of age-restricted content and you can't modify guides. it works well for chatting with friends, but you can't use the group chats, which is why you might need to install "steam chat".

it's great for viewing your wishlist, for browsing the store or your library but if you want to actually buy anything - forget it. it will go through the whole process and then say "authenticating" (or if you choose paypal the login screen just continuously asks for your email and doesn't go anywhere) but doesn't authenticate and the button doesn't do anything if you try to force it manually. you can click on the button but nothing actually happens. like they forgot to attach the code to it.
won't work at all will not let me sign in or create a new account at all on mobile and it's stupid because it shouldn't require a pc to be used it should be the same equal difficulty to use

meh. can't pay over paypal because the login doesn't work. tested on iphone and android. after typing the email, it just resets and asks again for my mail address. other than that, pretty slow and i also don't understand why it was necessary to split the messenger from it. so just good enough to have it installed, but not really usable.
i got logged out , now i can't log back in and no recovery options work at all so i'm not unable to use steam

i've made countless posts about this on steam, it's being ignored, despite it being a problem everyone can relate too. when typing, it's too easy to accidentally activate one of the buttons which will redirect you to another page and you will lose everything you wrote. could this bar, just be covered by the keyboard instead? or perhaps vanish all together if the person is typing? this is a really annoying issue and astonishing that it was designed like this in the first place.

seems to cause a high load and is buggy when playing videos full screen to the extent that i have to close the app. controls are too small. buttons need to be clicked twice. cannot complete a purchase with 3 different methods because the forwarding does not work... edit: months later, payment via direct pay, paypal etc are still not working. shopping basket is not synchronized to browser and everything needs to be added manually. edit almost another year later the payment process is still buggy

i had to make this review after the latest update. used to be a 5 star app and now it takes forever to find what i'm looking for. horrendously unintuitive ui and frustrating to deal with. i give it 2 stars because it technically works, but is painful to deal with now. i even removed the 2 factor authentication from my account just so i don't have to deal with it anymore. uninstalled until major changes occur.

the new app freezes all the time and loses its context when doing so, making you randomly jump to the home page. paying with paypal is not possible - it just keeps asking for the mail address (i have firefox as default browser, maybe that's the reason, some devs tend to test with chrome only). i don't care about the ui, so all "improvements" were to the worse for me.
very buggy and non-functional lately.

it's better than it was on rework launch, but almost everything i actually used this for is gone, hidden, or less usable. no chat. broken back button. stuff won't load consistently. the ui feels unintutive. steam guard insisting repeatedly i give access to my camera instead of using a code is irritating. it takes longer to load anything. wishlist still does not have any cover pictures. i am not downloading 2 separate apps for steam chat.

the fact that i can't purchase steam gift cards *in the app* feels like a major security issue. i'm already signed in on steam, but you want me to leave the app and sign in somewhere that's almost certainly less secure so other applications can access and skim my credit card info? i don't think so. why am i forced to compete this transaction in a browser when everything i should need exists in the steam app? why is this system deliberately made to be less secure?

does the job, more or less. but if using a browser, probably for a good reason, don't want to be asked at every single breath if i want to use the app instead. also there's no option to see which dlc's are owned in the library, which is kinda needed for games delisted from the store

it was actually good for a while but now i can't purchase games using paypal, it just asks for my email address on pp login, no prompt for my password. steam blame paypal and vice versa, so for now i have to pause the app, login on chrome, un-pause the app to verify, then finally purchase. it's ****ing ridiculous.

i can't get on, i've been at it for half an hour, i'm on a computer, it should work, i've put in my memorised password and username, over, and over, and it keeps telling me it isn't working, imma miss out on the game sale. i know i put it in right, i've done it 100 times already!! nvm, i got it, but still, tell people you can't put capital letters in their names, i got so frustrated!

like the desktop client, it's clunky and glitchy. i can't make purchases in the mobile app at all, it either errors out or just hangs indefinitely with a "click here if nothing happens" button, which also does nothing when clicked. steam guard via the app mostly works nowadays, but for a long time was also incredibly unreliable. so i guess it's mostly useful if you want an app to browse the steam store?
makes no sense it doesn't have chat function and it's main steam app.. otherwise good

do yourself a favour and turn off opening supported links with this app. every time i try to make a purchase on the web i get redirected to this app. every, single, time, i try to buy something with this it always ends with an issue. they take the money, game is not added to the library, have to re-purchase the game (after basket unlocks as the payment gets stuck) and then get my money back a few days later after the payment settles and realised it's been cancelled by steam. it's ridiculous.

great for logging into steam deck. good for browsing store. unfortunately can't buy through it. the paypal screen just keeps asking for an email address. tried using a card instead but that failed after authorization. the reason i want to use this is for convenience with a password manger but it doesn't work. annoyingly it has forced itself as defaults for links so i can't even use a browser instead. only option left is to uninstall.

okay, so there is issue. i can't even get in steam. i try making a account, but it won't let me in. it keeps saying "you need a valid email address to get in", even though i'm putting in a email address that i have used for 3 years. also, i have used the app before, and it's a broken mess. it's clunky, buggy, and glitchy. i would rather play sonic 06 for 10 hours straight than trying to figure out how to work this app. 2/5.

games are amazing, and the limited time price halving is so amazing. chat and connecting with other players is difficult, and i just couldn't understand the real problem when signing in on steam chat. too difficult where i just don't understand the problem but i'll still use steam because of the games it offers.
too much loading even on high speed internet

i can purchase games and download them to my machine through the app which is nice. however, sometimes when i go to order something they ask me to sign in, while i am signed in. then i have gotten a something went wrong error and then asked to sign in again. i have done this dance 12 times today and cannot complete my purchase. i tried to contact support to resolve this issue and trying to find where i go has been a hassle and i still haven't found the correct avenue for me to go.

please update this app the steam app used to be fine, but right now, it's nearly unusable. they've added some cool updates over the years and made the steam app look pretty nice. but the state the app is in on android right now is just unbearable. it constantly crashes when you open it or use it in any way. usually, i can't even get to the thing i opened the app for because it has already crashed. seriously, there's no reason for this app to be this unstable. please fix.

have been having problem,my email (which i used to create the account) says it's the wrong one,tried getting help by asking for the password,nothing,nor from my number (which i set up too at the start) nor from my email,so i couldn't get my password (that i couldn't remember) now im stuck for half an hour to "return later" or "try a different method" (that doesn't even make me do),if you fix this feature i would problably give it 4 stars but i have lost my account and got more problems retrievin

so buggy. navigation is all over the place and causes issues. if i add something to my cart and continue shopping and i gesture back or something, the cart button disappears so you can't access your cart. i have to close the app and re-open it to see the cart button with my items in it. that's just one of the many issues.

it was good before, really not a single bad thing, but after the overhaul there isn't a day without "there was a problem loading this web content", errors, crashes, loading different things than clicked (say, yesterday i couldn't even get into profile, had to force quit the app). can we just have the older version back? i see most of the reviews complain about the overhaul. edit: forgot to mention the separate chat app "feature". seriously? just get us back the old app.

well steam, i used to pay a subscription monthly with my debit card. then i purchased a game with a credit card and i see you've switched my subscription over to the credit card. i go to see what's up with that, and it appears you can only accept one method of payment at a time! how very lame of you.

i used to love my steam app because it was an all-in-one solution for everything steam related. why chat has been removed is a complete mystery to me and you now have to install a separate app just to use it. as the steam chat application currently sits with just 1.2 stars, i think that says everything we need to know. highly suggest turning off auto-update and rolling back to the old version. give back to us the same kind of support we've given the steam platform year after year!

great for looking at games. but don't try to buy games through this app. twice i tried to buy games through this app. twice i paid and it took me back to the front page. no game, no email with proof of purchase nothing. but money was taken out of my account. i don't know if there is something wrong with the app, but i have since uninstalled it.

for the most part it's fine for chatting to steam friends and steam guard features. however the app is useless for buying games. purchases rarely work and you end up with pending outgoing transactions from your bank and having to wait for days for them to clear. steam support say it's a banking issue when it's clearly this app thats the issue as so many people have the same problem. i don't have this issue with any other apps that are regularly used to make purchases elsewhere.

after the update that changed the layout, the app is not working properly anymore. the store sometimes fails to load, and at one point when i wanted to make a purchase, it immediately failed because a pop-up window for my bank was blocked. at least the log-in authentication functions work properly.

the app is so laggy, it's hard to use a lot of the time. half the time my inventory won't load, and the community market is a pain to open. everything works so slowly. but, at the same time, i have to have the app, so it makes my experience a very slow one. only something to expect from such a lazy company.

i expected that this app would be more convenient, however is it not. the only good thing that i would say about the app was buying games or maybe adding funds into the steam wallet if you're far away from your desktop. while for the other features, i might say that it could be broken or maybe not even suitable for a mobile device.

the sms code that was sent to me was not functioning when i fill the field with it. i was trying to remove my old number and change it with my new mobile number. the steam app has so many problems and the navigation of it is really slow. and when you confirm something via email and it automatically prompt you to the app anf it says error. hopefully the team prod will fix this asap.

it's wild to me that the same corporation that created the steam deck and entire os for it made this app. if you are in the store and look at a game and want to go back by hitting your phone's back button, it will minimize the app. imagine shopping at a store and when you pick something up then put it down an employee comes to kick you out of the store. giving it two stars because i'd like to think if it wasn't for that the app might be cool but i don't know because it keeps kicking me out.

the app went from being a secure program that provided me with a time-sensitive code for logging in to an app that is always logged into my account and provides access to my wallet. thanks for the changes now making it the least secured app on my phone.

marketplace inventory doesn't work. the only reason i got this app was to remove the market hold for items i want to sell from my inventory. i activated the authenticator and went through all the security protocols. when i click "sell an item" from my inventory, it starts loading my items and invetntory. the problem is that it loads forever! i never get to see my inventory and sell an item. basically this app is useless to m"e now and i regret even bothering with steam authenticator

the new update looks good and all, but it's much heavier now to run and everytime i want to open a new tab like for example community market, it says "there was a problem loading this content" event though my connection is strong. and what i hate the most is the chat is now separated from the original steam app and instead i have to download an extra app "steam chat" from the play store.

while useful, the app has several major issues. the worst of which is that it has a dangerously insecure authentication implementation for paypal that exposes your paypal authentication credentials details to steam, and third party attackers. there is no reason i should be forced to give my paypal password to steam. they should instead open an external browser, and via redirection end up back in the app at the end of the authentication flow (pkce). this implementation is lazy and dangerous.

never loads content from phone notifications ("there was a problem loading this web content"). paypal payments don't work. don't get me wrong, i love steam but those two bugs make the app's usefulness severely borked. not sure why they're not fixed as i'm not the only one to report them.

suspicious permissions (phone). no e-mail support. poor chat functionality (constantly jumping when someone's writing, notified about new messages but they don't show up when i open the app). breaks steam links. developer seems very invested in censoring user reviews. says "too many login failures" when i enter the correct credentials. otherwise good.

you need to remortgage your house to purchase the very expensive addons which seem to be a con to make you part with more cash. the sale items are not on sale because when you press purchase, it reverts back to the original full price so be very careful before you press pay. great games but extremely expensive.

typically the app is fine. somtimes the interface sucks and is difficult to use. but the thing that sets me over the edge, is that my google pixel is not being recognized as a phone on the app. so when i log in, it requires the code on my mobile authenticator... which is on my phone, that it's asking for authication to get into. you see the problem?

the ui is basically useless for navigation. most of the ui is side-swiping tiles for games. only 1.5 games are in screen at a time, but the sensitivity and acceleration are so high that a tiny swipe sends you 2-3 games forward in the list. and it's difficult to precisely move forward just one. then, when you have gotten into a game page but decide to go back, sometimes the back button on nav bar just exits the app and there's no way to get back to your search where you left off. very frustrating

trying to do a password recovery, every time i tab out to get the code from my email when i come back it's on the sign in screen. now i'm locked out. also it's nearly impossible to find anything. too many pages and sections, and the search bar is basically useless. you can type in the exact thing you're looking for and it'll be at the bottom of a 10 page list, behind tons of other things that aren't even relevant to what you're searching for! extremely frustrating.

my gods is the app crashy and a hot mess. the overall functionality just isn't there and it runs really slow and i couldn't begin to tell you why. it does let you slowly buy games and look at your collection after it crashes twice. i also don't like that custom artwork for games is only available on the desktop version and that they accept some questionable games that are scammy to say the least. it's overall not very convinent and it drives me nuts because that was the point of the app.

buggy and broken system. steam forces you to download this if you want to trade your items on the marketplace. set it up and then wait 7 days to see if restrictions are now lifted. if it doesn't work after that (which it doesn't), try tweaking a few settings, then wait another 7 days to see if steam on your computer says "you've had steam guard set up for 7 days" yet. still no? great, try re-installing and then wait another 7 days. grim experience.
